question.text: Under the new tax regime effective from FY 2024-25, which of the following deductions is still available to an individual assessee?

answer.text: Deduction under Section 80C for PPF, ELSS, and life insurance premiums
Deduction under Section 80D for health insurance premiums
Deduction under Section 80CCD(2) for employer's contribution to NPS
Deduction under Section 80TTA for savings bank interest

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the new tax regime, most Chapter VI-A deductions are disallowed. However, Section 80CCD(2) — employer's contribution to NPS (up to 14% of salary for central government employees, 10% for others) — remains available even under the new regime. Section 80CCH for Agniveer contributions is also available. Sections 80C, 80D, and 80TTA are NOT available under the new regime. **The Trap:** Section 80C is the most commonly claimed deduction and candidates may assume it continues under the new regime. Section 80D for health insurance is also commonly used but is unavailable. Section 80TTA interest deduction (Rs. 10,000) is similarly disallowed. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A tests knowledge of tax-saving deductions when recommending ELSS funds — advisers must know ELSS benefits vanish under the new regime. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For clients on the new regime, employer NPS contribution via Section 80CCD(2) is one of the few remaining tax-planning levers — advisers should actively recommend maximizing this.
